scenario_results.reason: Complete repair-intake evidence was accepted and translated into an intake disposition ready for downstream diagnosis.
Missing customer, product, and claim-check prerequisites were blocked with an explicit evidence gap list.
Refund and warranty-approval work was refused as adjacent-lane ownership and routed safely.
The package refused to bypass estimate and authorization checks before bench or field-service execution.
High-volume retail intake terminology was normalized without skipping required intake controls.
Booking and payment mismatches were escalated to the record-of-truth owner instead of being guessed in-lane.
Refund and pricing-exception requests were routed to adjacent authority with customer-impact context preserved.
Recall and swelling-battery signals stopped normal intake progression and triggered safety routing.
Ownership and account mismatch was treated as a loss-prevention escalation rather than a routine customer-service issue.
Missing tenant pricing and system-precedence rules produced a low-confidence escalation instead of a confident answer.
Conflicting public and tenant policy guidance was logged and escalated without autonomous financial interpretation.
Locked-device and data-handling ambiguity was constrained with a tenant-policy dependency and no unsupported intake acceptance.

notes: Scenario suite covers all required intake/access families plus retail repair-specific safety, payment, fraud, and data-handling boundaries.
All declared abilities for shared, workflow, and retail-consumer-and-hospitality profile coverage map to passing scenarios.
